aggregatable.description: Whether this field can be aggregated on all indices.

indices.description: The list of indices where this field has the same type family, or null if all indices have the same type family for the field.

meta.description: Merged metadata across all indices as a map of string keys to arrays of values. A value length of 1 indicates that all indices had the same value for this key, while a length of 2 or more indicates that not all indices had the same value for this key.

non_aggregatable_indices.description: The list of indices where this field is not aggregatable, or null if all indices have the same definition for the field.

non_searchable_indices.description: The list of indices where this field is not searchable, or null if all indices have the same definition for the field.

searchable.description: Whether this field is indexed for search on all indices.

inference.description: Whether this field is an inference field, meaning a field that automatically performs inference (for example, `semantic_text` fields), on all indices.

non_inference_indices.description: The list of indices where this field is not an inference field, or null if all indices have the same definition for the field.

metadata_field.description: Whether this field is registered as a metadata field.

time_series_dimension.description: Whether this field is used as a time series dimension.

time_series_metric.description:
Contains metric type if this fields is used as a time series
metrics, absent if the field is not used as metric.

non_dimension_indices.description:
If this list is present in response then some indices have the
field marked as a dimension and other indices, the ones in this list, do not.

metric_conflicts_indices.description:
The list of indices where this field is present if these indices
don’t have the same `time_series_metric` value for this field.
